WelcomeModal.title: Welcome — AI Candidate (PoC)
WelcomeModal.description: Reopen anytime via Disclaimer in the header.
WelcomeModal.sectionBuiltBody: Next.js (App Router), next-intl EN/PT, themes, shadcn-style UI. Three columns: candidates, stage + chat, profile.
whatCanItems: Demo candidates & profile; add persona (lost on refresh). Text bar → local chat only.
Sessions / History / Settings = layout mocks. EN/PT + theme in the header.
WelcomeModal.sectionMocksBody: No auth. Interview not persisted server-side. Mic / pause / stop are visual only. LLM route is a stub until a real client is wired.
plannedItems: Database + APIs for users, sessions, transcripts; auth & workspaces.
Real LLM (see below) and speech: STT + TTS for the candidate.
WelcomeModal.choicesIntro: Swap implementations via UserRepository and LlmClient.
dbOptions: Postgres / MySQL / SQLite; Drizzle, Kysely, or plain SQL. PoC: JSON file for /api/users.
llmOptions: OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Gemini; or self-hosted (Ollama, vLLM, OpenAI-compatible).
voiceOptions: Web Speech API for quick tests; cloud STT/TTS (Google, Azure, AWS, ElevenLabs, …).
WelcomeModal.sectionResearchIntro: Brief conclusions from docs/VOICE_AND_LLM_OPTIONS.md (prices and free tiers change—check vendors).
researchItems: Portuguese STT: set pt-BR or pt-PT explicitly; quality swings with accent, mic, and noise—test on your own interview clips, not a single benchmark.

Try STT for free: run Whisper locally (e.g. faster-whisper, whisper.cpp). Browser Web Speech costs nothing but is often weak for PT. For streaming, shortlist cloud STT (Azure, Google, Deepgram, AssemblyAI) and compare on the same audio.
Cheap LLM PoC: Google AI Studio (Gemini), Groq free tier, or Ollama on your machine. When paying, smaller/faster models (e.g. Gemini Flash, GPT-4o-mini) are usually enough for short replies.

TTS: free/offline Piper or robotic browser speech; lower-cost cloud neural voices (Azure, Google, Polly); more natural speech tends to cost more (e.g. ElevenLabs). Match voice locale to pt-BR vs pt-PT.
STT, LLM, and TTS are separate: better recognition does not fix a weak model or a mismatched voice.

SettingsModal.title: Workspace settings
SettingsModal.description: What the product spec will let you tune: AI behavior, audio/voice, session defaults, and workspace UI.
SettingsModal.intro: This PoC is layout only — persistence and APIs will be wired in a later phase.
SettingsModal.sectionAiHint: Model choice, sampling temperature, and extra system instructions for the virtual candidate and assistant.

SettingsModal.temperatureHint: Higher values produce more varied wording. Not persisted yet.
